summary: Fast canary live comparative validation scenarios for frequent iteration loops.

scenarios.name: smoke-chorus-focus
scenarios.analyzePrompt: Analyze the current song for sections, beat windows, phrase cues, and energy shifts.
scenarios.strongPrompt: Design a single Chorus 1 concept anchored to the beat grid for Snowman and Star. Keep Snowman as the focal read and use Star as support. Do not rewrite the whole show.
scenarios.weakPrompt: Treat Chorus 1 as one broad even look across the yard with no real focal hierarchy. Keep the effect language simple and uniform.

scenarios.name: smoke-composition-pass
scenarios.analyzePrompt: Analyze the current song for sections, beat windows, phrase cues, and energy shifts.
scenarios.strongPrompt: Use the perimeter props to frame the song while the centerpiece props take over the key reveals and choruses.
scenarios.weakPrompt: Keep the full song visually even so the perimeter and centerpiece props all share the same emphasis most of the time.

scenarios.name: smoke-phrase-subtlety
scenarios.analyzePrompt: Analyze the current song for sections, beat windows, phrase cues, and energy shifts.
scenarios.strongPrompt: In the Bridge, hold the breath before the phrase release and shape the lift with subtler within-phrase timing instead of filling the whole section evenly.
scenarios.weakPrompt: In the Bridge, ignore the phrase release and fill the whole section with the same broad look from start to end.
